name: Black Bean Soup#2
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Slab bacon or 2 smoked ham hocks (1 1/2 to 2 lb)
1 1/2 cup Finely chopped, seeded and cored green peppers
1 can (19-oz) tomatoes peeled and diced with juice about 2 1/2 cups
2 tbsp Finely chopped fresh coriander, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Place beans, bacon or ham hocks, water, and celery salt in a heavy kettle.
2. Bring to boil; cover and simmer for about 2 1/2 hours or until beans are thoroughly tender.
3. Remove bacon or ham hocks; set aside. Drain beans and reserve along with meat and cooking liquid.
4. There should be about 6 cups of beans and 4 cups of liquid. Add enough broth to make 6 cups of liquid.
5. Combine the beans with liquid in a large bowl.
6. Heat oil in a heavy kettle; add peppers, onions, garlic, and cumin. Cook, stirring until onions are wilted.
7. Add tomatoes and vinegar. Let simmer for about 15 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, remove and discard skin from bacon or skin and bones from ham hocks. Chop the meat; set aside.
9. Add meat mixture to cooked tomato mixture. Add chopped garlic and coriander.
10. Simmer until thoroughly heated. Serve in soup bowls; garnish with sieved hard cooked egg, if desired.
